propositions: Operation certificates migrate from per-clone symmetric HMAC self-attestations to asymmetric Ed25519 signatures attributed to the signing environment and verifiable by any third party against that environment's public key.

A project can optionally require a certificate from a specific trusted environment for a gate, pinning trusted-environment public keys out-of-band in .rebar/trusted_environments.yaml on the Gerrit-gated code branch rather than the auto-pushed tickets branch.
The merge gate verifies the required-environment certificate over the merged log by extending the shipped verify-identity.yaml gate rather than building a parallel merged-log walker.
Certificates bind {ticket id, material fingerprint, merged-log position as a main commit SHA} so they cannot be replayed onto a different ticket or mutated state.

Environment-key rotation uses explicit-SHA era-scoped validity (added_at_commit/revoked_at_commit compared via git merge-base --is-ancestor), reusing the commit-ancestry era rule and key_not_valid_at_era verdict rather than the tickets-branch event resolver.

A trusted rebar-mcp remote-execution path fetches authoritative ticket state and code-at-commit itself and signs the verdict over that, so client-supplied doctored inputs cannot forge a passing verdict, while the default local path stays offline.
